algorithms.title: H's & T's Systematic Arrest Workup
algorithms.description: A structured checklist approach to identifying and treating reversible causes during cardiac arrest.
steps: Cardiac arrest identified — begin CPR and deliver first defibrillation as indicated.
While CPR continues, team lead initiates H's & T's checklist with one team member assigned to investigate.
Hypovolemia: Check clinical history for hemorrhage, assess IVC on ultrasound, give IV fluids or blood products.
Hypoxia: Confirm advanced airway in place, ensure adequate ventilation with 100% FiO₂, check ETCO₂.
Hydrogen ion (acidosis): Assess ventilation adequacy; address the underlying cause rather than empiric bicarbonate.
Hypo-/Hyperkalemia: Check bedside potassium if available; for hyperkalemia, give calcium chloride, bicarbonate, insulin/glucose, albuterol.
Hypothermia: Measure core temperature; do not declare futility until ≥32°C; active rewarming.
Tension pneumothorax: Assess breath sounds, tracheal position, JVD; decompress if suspected.
Tamponade: Assess heart sounds, JVD; bedside ultrasound; pericardiocentesis if confirmed.
Toxins: Review medication list and clinical context; give specific antidotes when available.
Thrombosis (pulmonary): Consider massive PE — thrombolysis or embolectomy.
Thrombosis (coronary): Consider acute MI — early defibrillation and coronary reperfusion.
Reassess after each 2-minute CPR cycle — new or previously missed reversible causes may emerge.
Document findings and interventions for CQI review.

algorithms.title: Refractory VF/pVT Escalation Pathway
algorithms.description: Stepwise management when VF/pVT persists despite standard defibrillation attempts.
steps: VF/pVT identified → Deliver biphasic shock (200 J) and resume CPR immediately.
After 2 minutes of CPR → rhythm check; still VF/pVT → shock again (200 J), resume CPR.
Give epinephrine 1 mg IV/IO during CPR (as soon as feasible).
After 3rd shock → rhythm check; still VF/pVT → REFRACTORY VF/pVT.
Give amiodarone 300 mg IV/IO bolus (or lidocaine 1–1.5 mg/kg if amiodarone unavailable).
REASSESS CPR quality: rate 100–120/min, depth ≥2 in, full recoil, minimize interruptions, check ETCO₂.
REASSESS compressor: rotate if ≥2 minutes elapsed.
Consider vector-change defibrillation (reposition pads) or DSED (Class 2b).
REASSESS H's & T's: tension pneumo, tamponade, toxins, PE, hypoxia, hypothermia.
Deliver shock (200 J) with antiarrhythmic on board; resume CPR.
If still refractory: consider magnesium 1–2 g IV (polymorphic VT), additional antiarrhythmic, or consider ECPR if available.
Continue cycling: 2 min CPR → rhythm check → shock → reassess, until ROSC or futility criteria met.
